answer:
To find \( P(D \mid TP) \), we use **Bayes' Theorem**:

\[
P(D \mid TP) = \frac{P(TP \mid D) \cdot P(D)}{P(TP)}
\]

Given:
- \( P(D) = 0.05 \),
- \( P(TP \mid D) = 0.99 \),
- \( P(\text{not } D) = 0.95 \), so \( P(\text{FP} \mid \text{not } D) = 1 - 0.97 = 0.03 \).

The total probability of testing positive is:
\[
P(TP) = P(TP \mid D)P(D) + P(TP \mid \text{not } D)P(\text{not } D) = 0.99 \times 0.05 + 0.03 \times 0.9
